In France, it exist a form od politeness and etiquette, this is some pointers. For example it's rude and forbidden spitting in the street and impolite slurpping in public. The punctuality is important, you're expected to arrive at time or with five or ten minutes in advance at a business meeting. When you're invited at someone's home it's polite to arrive with a smal gift with five or ten minutes in late. Moreover it's impolite to phone after10 p.m except for good friends. In the public transport you're supposed to give your seat to ederly people or expectant mother. In the elevator, public transport it's impolite staring people. When you arrive in a room you supposed to bow people but not always shake hand however it's an usage.

text: It's well-known that people dislikes rules. However, they are necessary to make the consequences of our acts bearable fot the others. One advice says that you should not do to the others what you would dislike they do to you. Law is useful therein. By drawing the line, it allows people to be respectful towards the others and to avoid physical and psychologic injuries. Do you imagine a world without any driving regulations ? I would be afraid at every corner and you too. And we would finally be okay to create laws. Laws are necessary to live together even if they seem to be strict and sometimes too restrictive at an individual level.

topic: Signing a waiver to go skydiving
text: In my country, nowadays, smoking is prohibited in almost public places. When I was younger, I remember that two choices were possible if you traveled by train: you could have a seat in smoking or not smoking compartment. You could smoke everywhere: at a TV show for example,  guests sometimes smoked, even the presenter. I had a teacher who smoked in the classroom. This seems unthinkable today. I totally agree with the actual limitation of smoking, I think it's more enjoyable, healthy. What makes me definitely nostalgic is the atmosphere in bars, when I look at old movies on jazz musicians. Our world is certainly healthier but... slightly annoying.

text: At the very beginning of the meeting the CEO required the attention of the assistans. She read the agenda and said that there was three subjet to discuss. After that she thanks the people for coming and started to expose the purpose of the meeting. They faced problems with the new merger, mainly because of the missing comunication. I have been living in the South West of France since 2000 and I have discovered that each region has its custom which makes it special. So, I'd like to speak about a traditional dance called' La Danse sur Echasses' of that agricultural region where I am living. In ancient times, because it  was a marshy area, when shepherds were guardering their flocks of sheep, they had to use stilts for walking. Thanks to that elevating position of moving, they could take advantage of it. First, even in wetlands, they could easily look after their flocks. Then, they could see over the hills and so far away in the country that they never got lost. Unfortunately,little by littlle,shepherds,herds,woods began to disappear with the development of industrialization. Despite of that, the traditional stilts weren't all-forgotten and  became an important accessory of dancing! Nowadays, if you like traditional folk entertainment, you can attend an open-air show. What an engaging performance! I love watching these kind of dancers who are wearing bright colored costumes . How fascinating they are when they began to dance round in circles with those strange shoes!

Let me tell us about the new self-helped book " How to Make a Career out of Courage " by Judith's Wight's. The strength of this book is that it helps workers to consider what interest they find in their profession or could find in another one. The weakness of this book is that it's not very realistic in the context of crisis and difficulties to get a work. But finally, it will help some people to change a difficult work which is boring with another which will be more exciting ! It claims that the search for the perfect career is a lot easier that it seems !

In my office, I was always working very hard and I didn't have any promotion! One day, I needed some advice of other colleague. Her desk was near the director's desk and she was very appreciated by him. She told me I would meet the director to tell him my request. So, I did! I was very satisfied because he received me very well and listened to me with attention. So, he gave me a positive answer. I was very happy and I thanked him for his great help. In the same time, I decided to show better all that I was doing in my work and explain with more and more details to value my goals. It was successful! What wasn't my surprise when he promoted me!

I'm a young entrepreneur. I'm just 27 years old and I've just finished my studies in Management. I love read books, newspapers. Before, I bought so much books in libraries when I wanted to read one. Antismoking efforts are hard to avoid nowadays in France. Since 5 years, it's not allowed to smoke in a public place. Smoking in a bar, a restaurant is prohibited. If the regulations were violated, smokers faced a fine of 68 euros. From a political point of view, this law had good intentions: to protect all the citizen from an dangerous atmosphere. We breathe better. Non-smokers are happy: they win:"it stinks of tobacco in here" they used to say. It's finished, pubs smell good, an so healthy air that a baby could have his night there. Even when it's raining, smokers goes out, in front of the bar or restaurant to continue to puff on their cigarettes. During the evening, every time we want to smoke, we quit the table, we get dressed again. New laws, new habits. A kind of smoker's brotherhood exists on sidewalks in the night: conviviality is not inside but outside! If you ask me, I love to light up when I have a drink in hand and it's more complicated, less comfortable now. In fact, going out "somewhere" it's to stay up in the street and the cold.

There are a lot of situation which are so awkward in life but if I have to pick one, I would speak about the party we had with Felicie and Pablo. At that time they were still together because now they broke up. It was in the winter time, six people were invited, in fact three couples. We started with appetizer and had a drink. But the situation quickly became awkward. Felicie was everytime very rude with Pablo, telling him very offensive things. For example she criticized the clothes he wears, the movie he likes to watch, the music he enjoys listening and last but not least said horrible things about his physical appearance. The other people, my wife and I had been so embarrassed that we had had just one desire: to escape. We didnt want to be rude with Pablo so we  stayed but it was very difficult to feel comfortable in that kind of situation. Finally, one years later they decided to break up and today I really think that it was the best solution for them. I hope we wont live again a party like that.

Is gender equality exist in France in 2014? I don't think so, in my country, there still are gender differences. Although, times are changed. The most important difference is between men and women's salery. Men win 10% more than women to the same work. I think that, same work, same salery. However, of course, men and women can't doing all the same job. There are physical differences who can't be changed. On the physical side, gender equality is not important because it's a fact, even if there are exceptions, I guess. Nowadays, roles between males and females are shared. The man takes care about his children, as the woman. He cookes too. I think equality can be improve, more over in the minds.

Last year,mid June,  it was a week day, I was wainting for my 8.10 train, for unknown reason this train was concealed a the very last moment. The next one scheduled 10 minutes later, was fully packed. I jumped on it but no more free avaible seat so I stood in the middle of the carriage with no possiblitily to grap me somewhere in case of a sudden tremor. Then with no understanding reason the train stopped suddenly the driver used his brakes fiercely, all of us in the middle felt,many were scared some screamed, others got a fit of giggles the situation was awkward but also funny, anybody was hurt just few bruises, and dust on clothes. Most of the people in the middle of the carriage were teenagers, students. Once up the train started again, the driver apologizes and said through his microphone the problem was because a fire on the tracks. We arrive to the terminus 20 minutes later all in good shape.

text: Hi Carrie, You missed the presentation of John that candidates on the new CEO position and I would like you give you my impressions on his speech. First, he gave a survey on his previous research topics and one thing he did well was to situate his work among the domain of work and compare it with other approaches. I had to admire his pedagogy when presenting a very simple graphic that summarize a non trivial research area. Then he gradually explained the specificity of his work and finally presented his algorithms that seem very innovative. However I have not be convinced by his speaking ability, too familarly most of the time for a future CEO. I also found his presentation too much technical such that most of the audience didn't understand the key points. I would have preferred that he presented how he would manage the institute when coming at the head of the team and how he could help people work together despite their various research themes. He did it at the end but it was so simplistic that he didn't convinced me at all.

Hello Harry, When you travel in the South West of France and arrive to Arcachon, you will be invited for a dinner by a few friends of mine. I understand that you are a trifle puzzled because each country has its own etiquette but don't worry. Staple rules are the same all over the world. Therefore, I just will give you some advices about a few manners which are perhaps specific for French people. First, you must arrive on time, neither earlier, nor later. As you know, a little gift from South Africa will be appreciated by your host; if they have children, don't forget that parents always like when guests have a thought for them. During the dinner, you will be in danger to be surprised by the food because eating habits are different, but don't bother! If your host serve a typical-like meal, for example oysters ( a speciality of Arcachon), he will know that it's really special and tell you that you may eat no one. Nevertheless, if you can, taste them: he will be happy by seeing that you are interested in our local food. In a nutshell, the golden rule consists in traveling being open minded and showing interest toward others.%%

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: I was responsible for the coordination between several teams  for the development of a software. The operational officer presented his requests : a reliable product, with no bugs and which works quickly. They didn't have time to lose because of a lack of IT efficiency. But the accountant warned us about the costs' limits : we had to respect the budget granted for these suppliers ! That's why I proposed to automate only the essential tasks, and to carry out manually all the other works. The CEO and the other members accepted my proposal.

What roles do males and females have in my country, I mean, in France? I think relationships between men and women exist with no contentious, in France. Of course, male chauvinism appears sometimes, but generally, males and females share tasks at home. No need having written rules, nor strict agendas. They are compatible and accept to change their role if it's necessary, but the problem is elsewhere. If you look at a picture of parliment. You can see the women are under-represented. A shame! A lot of improvements would be necessary to obtain an equal representation  males/females in high responsability jobs.

Who could say why there are still so many gender differences in France in 2013? The most visible gender difference is still at work. Most women work today but is is still difficult for them to reach the same level in the hierarchy and to have the same salary for an equal job. Mentality has changed of course. But most women have to take care of their children and it is not easy for them to invest themselves at work as much as men. Nowadays, women have more freedom but as a consequency, there are more and more women who are raising their children alone and are more prone to insecurity. However, which women would like to go back in the 1990s? Today, there are gender equality in the studies and women have much more opportunities than before. Gender differences are decreasing but there are still much room for improvements.

text: I took the floor in a medical congress to present a new organisational method to bring some help to patients. I had fifteen minutes to present 5 slides. I began with a joke about the place where the congress were. Then, I presentes myself and the three points that I was going to talk about : the key issues, how is working this new organisation, and the first results. I told the congressmates to keep their questions for the end of the presentation. After that, I went on with the slides and explain the differents points. I took care with my speed speech, because I knew I use to speak to fast. I pause sometimes after some important issue to be more compelling. After the last slide presenting a chart of the first results, I told they could ask any questions they would now or by mail.

Nowadays, if we want to be succesful, I think we have to work in teams. So the first thing is to build an effective team. How to build it? First we have to look for the good manager. A good manager is a person who can effectively listen to all members, discuss with all of them and bring up the better of each one. He must be impressive and a good communicator. He must analyse each idea and try to incorporate some of them in the company project.  He must keep in touch with all the team members but be careful: too many mails, too many meetings, too many calls can make the members boring. The contacts between each other must be precise, useful and short. . Each team member has different and unique skills. The main role for the manager is to discover what each team member can do. On the other hand the team members must commit to the project. To end, I think the leader must find ways after work to socialize with everyone, to make the members feeling as a family. Why not create team buildings outside the company?

In France, it's a tradition to invite people for a meal or a party. To make a first good impression, people are supposed to bring wine or flowers. You should also wait to eat until your hosts seat and start eating. It's very important, even if it seems obvious, not to speak the mouth full. In my country the meal is very important. For us it's a time where we can share thoughts, discuss with our family, have a good time. So if you or your child get up without saying the reason, or for the children without asking for an autorisation, it could appear very rude for the other guests. And if you want to show how respectful are your children, it's important to make them follow this advice. If you don't like what is in your plate, it's better to say that you can't eat because for example "I'm sorry but I can't digest that" than to say I don't like it. And the better way to please your host, is to eat the meal without saying anything, mostly when you don't like it. Last but not least, smile and enjoy the party.%%

I don't have a lot of  cash but I'm getting by. The biggest part of my monthly expenses is to reimburse the mortgage and the utilities bills for electricity, water, taxes. The second part is for my horse. Indeed, he lives in the stables and I have to pay his rent ! It is expensive enough and it increases faster than the cost of living. The third part is all the outgoings for ma car. I do many kilometers every day for my job. So, I have many big bills from the garage owner ! Finally, I have to pay food and all the stuff for everyday. At the end of the month, I wait with impatience my salary !

text: The increase of technology in our lives this last years has completely changed our way of life: even in a restaurant or during some important event you stay connected and focused on your smartphones, watching the news, sending sms, reading your emails.. There were supposed to allow us to have a more social life, but the fact is that we are now so dependent of this machines and of internet that we are not preoccupied anymore of the real life: meeting new people face to face, enjoying a discussion without looking at your phone every two minutes, find your way without a GPS, reading newspapers instead of an application from a newspaper... We forgot how to stay connected to real people in the real life. At the end in one way technology has helped us, on the other hand, it had make us completely dependant on something we might not be able to control anymore on day.

text: I recently attended a presentation about the urban development in France.The presenter was the former mayor of Dijon, an important city in France. He was an excellent speaker, like most of the politicians. He spoked , without staring at his notes and looked at people around all the time.His opening was excellent. His first joke, wich worked well was tremendous to grab attention and he gave the audience a very clear plan. On the other hand, he did'nt emphasized enough his main ideas. One thing he could have done differently was to release more facts and figures. He remained at a level of generality. If he had given us more examples, more visuals, or more charts, we would have better remember his main ideas.  As he developped the consequences of urban deccay an the positive perspectives of urban renovation, he delivered a boring speech, without real cities of flesh and blood.I'm still not sure to have understood the purpose of his statement. In the conclusion, he failed to summarize her keypoints.I'm not sure we learned a lot.

In my country of origin there still are enormous differences between genders. It is about an ex-communist country with traditional expectations and stereotypes well seated. There is still an important percentage of women staying at home at raising children and men working, taking decisions and being the king of the house. It's already been more than 20 years since the political system changed and things are changing for the better, women are beginning to fight for their independence and they are also starting to have their carrier and feminist expectations. For now women are still being payed less than men, women are still forbidden to occupy men's positions, like engineering or important political positions and I think that we are not going to have a president woman in the next 20 years.

text: I would like to give my opinion on the presentation of this morning, regarding the future of our company. First of all, I have to admire how the presenter outlined our problems, it was really clear, and gave us a complete overview of the companies issues. One other thing he did well was to offer examples for each key point, as well as pie charts, which was extremely helpful to understand exactly were we are. However, I would have liked it if he'd used bigger or less complicated charts. To be honest, it did not really work as it was for such a small presentation. I either wasn't sure about some solutions he suggested at the end. I was expecting something different. But I am convinced he did a great presentation and gave us all the key points to modify our way to work in the future.

Actually I consider that my life is pretty good: I have a roof over my head, food on the table. I can watch, hear, walk along the street in a free country. However I have a job that makes sense, life can be hard and stressful. Something is definitely missing in our western society: people always complain about their life. More we have everything we need, more our dissatisfaction rise up. The high quality of our life -with all modern conveniences- doesn't seem to be enough to smile more! What a paradox! Perhaps I'm wrong but I just search now to simplify my way of life...I don't dream about more money, I appreciate a hearty meal shared with friends or family more than buy a new car.Feeling of happiness isn't measured by material possessions. My social life isn't hectic and demanding,I hate every kind of obligations. In business it's important to network whenever possible. Fortunately, my lifestyle is far from those problems. What I need is absolutly less stress. And more time. I like the idea of "degrowth", this movement that advocate "voluntary simplicity ".

Currently, I'm going to a computer programming school. But it wasn't always like this. My initial job was banker. Prior the economic crisis, I studied Finances to became a banker. When the crisis came up and bubble burst, I stayed one year searching a job. It was really difficult for me but I tried to stay courageous. Then, I did a call-center job for few months. Subsequently, I went to England and I worked as commis waiter to improve my English. Soon afterward, I found a job as banker at CIC bank and I worked there for 3 years. But unfortunately, Bank changed much during economic crisis and currently i don't like how this job became. I decided to stop my job to follow Computer programming course. Following my school, I hope to be able to work as computer programmer. I'm very passionate about Computer for my youngness and i hope to realize my dream.

text: There was fluctuation between 2004 to 2008 in international book sales.Between 2004-2005 there was a dramatic rise to a figure of 2 million followed by a sharp drop to about 2.75 million.In 2005 book sales fell sharply to about 1 million to a level of 2007 when there was a sharp decrease in sales of about half a million. National sales figures: Between 2004-2005 book sales rose sharply to 1 million. 2005-2006:There was a constant level of sales at 1 million. 2006-2007:sales increased dramatically to around 3 million followed by a sharp drop to one and half million.

Last year, I went with my family in Vende to go at "Puy du Fou". It's a leisure park whose there are many shows in different times. For instance, there are the circus games in antiquity or a spectacle where the vikings attack or again we could visit a french castle in Renaissance times. However, for me the best spectacle is no one of these but it's a show called "Cinscnie". "Cinscnie" is the biggest night show in the world, it last 1h40. There are 1200 actors and dancers with fireworks. This show is very beautiful and this is for all of these reasons that the show is unique.

I was working as a chief financial officer for my company since 5 years and everything was going well. Three months ago, we hired a trainee to help me close my financial year. This day he was in my office and wanted me to explain him how the work goes in our company and how all the staff is. He was sitting in front of me and I was standing up with the door in my back. After explaining all the processes of the work, it's come the time to describe the staff. I was describing our general manager when I noticed that I seemed to be quiet embarrassed. I stopped my description and looked behind me: my boss was just here and was looking at me. He was really very upset. In fact my description was a little bit exaggerated, and I was joking about the way he spoke. I tried to explain it to my boss, and after warning me, he told me that my joke was quiet funny. Finally the issue wasn't as grave as I feared.

I have a really good life. I take care of the people at their home and they seems to be happy. I'm not married and I live in a comfortable house near my parents, so I get to spend time with them. I do not have stress at work and that makes me fell in good health. I haven't time to take summers off. As far as money goes, I occasionally get a little nervous because I have to pay the bill for the house and it's quite expensive. Fortunately, I don't have to live from paycheck to paycheck. The fact is I've got everything I need and more. However, sometimes I fell that someone is missing. You see, I'm single and sometimes I get bored. I wish I had kids but I have to find a girl. Anyway, my social life is rather slow. I wish I had more parties to go on and spend more time in the clubbing. It would be nice if I could meet someone. I do not have mid-life crisis and for me life is good and I'm Learning to enjoy the good things everyday.

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: My Smartphone is my best friend; It and me are inseparable. At 7 o'clock, he said me "wake up". My first reaction is to throw it in the bedroom. But finally I take it and talk to him ! "what about the weather ?" "What are my appointements today ?". Since I bought it, I forgot all phone numbers, all addresses, the subway plan. Since I bought it, I have no pen, no paper in my bag. I need to purchase something ? I note it on my smartphone ! It is my memory ! Until the black day, when a black screen don't awaked me in the morning, when the same black screen has forgot my doctor's appointement and don't say me to buy milk. It the same day that I realized that I need seriously to begin a cure detox. Then my friend and me are separate all day from 9 pm. Until 7 am of course.

My wife and I went recently to a performance art. It was a special music show. It was special for many reasons. First, the performance took place in a barn, in a farm. The atmosphere was cheerful. There were two singers. The first one was a woman. She sang several sings very funny and she was joking between each songs. The second singer was a man. It was unique. He sang songs with differents instruments as trumpet, sax, or guitar. He was changing his dress between each song, to be closer with the kind of song he was singing. That was very funny. He also imitated some well known french singers. He did it very well. We laughed a lot. We have liked very much this performance. It was well worth the money. After this evening, we wanted to go on to others performances. We had stopped to go out until this evening, owing to our schedules very busy.

One of the most fascinating performing art in Paris is the the world renowned Opera ballet corp.It's the heir of the royal ballet but it has its currently form since the Second Empire. Around one hundred dancers leaded by the Etoiles play the most famous clasical ballets, like The Swan Lake or Nutcracker. It's a marvelous experience to see dancer moving on the floor as lightly as a bird. There absolutely nothing else like these women wearing tutus and pointe shoes playing the Pas de deux accompagnied by Tchakovsky's great music. The Marius Petipa's choreography is amazing, the costumes are exquisite, the body language of the dancers is marvelous. If you get in the right mood, you feel being in a dream, you feel even being a part of the dream. It's truly a masterpiece. It's well worth his money and his time. It's an unique testimony to the french culture and ancient european way of life. And the fabulous landmark of the Opera itself makes this unique. Most of people who have never been to a ballet befor want to go back to such a spectacle. But afterward, il they go and see another company, they can be a bit disappointed.%% French dancers are probably unrivaled in the world.

French companies are facing aggressive competition of German firms. In order to deal with this new situation, we need to carry out a strategic analysis. I mean, we should evaluate our strengths and weakness. The first thing is to build an analyst team bringing together employees from all departments of our company. Each one should make a list of issues which seem being relevant and making sense. Our job, as managers, would be to make sure that all the major questions are covered. Then I suggest to appoint one person to be in charge of every main topic. He or her should gather information and ideas in his or her department and summarize it. Once we'll have got back all the surveys, we'll be able to write a guideline on the main issues. This paper will have to be release as broadly as possible. To be effective, it will have to be reviewed frequently. When the entire staff will identify our strengths and weaknesses, it will become more aware of the threats and it will accept more easily to make sacrifices which necessarily includes working harder and longer. From my point of view, this analysis built by the whole staff is the best way to prevent upcoming troubles.%%

On a legal point of view, there remains in France no differences between men and women. They can both play key leading roles in business and politics. But we have to come to terms with the fact that there are fewer women having top management responsabilities. Why ? It's probably due to the weight of history, to an old tradition of women helping the professional life of their husband. But that doesn't explain why, at the same level, women earn in average ten or twenty percent less than their husband. They usually pass the same exams than men, sometimes more brightly. They have got the same skills and are offen very talented.  They are good at running people and business. But they don't get to the top. Today, when you work in politics or business at A Level, you almost never hear chauvinist talks or thaughts. it's more subtle and insidious.The glass ceiling exists, but nobody is able to say what are the true reasons to explain this deplorable mess.

Why there are the differences between the genders? I like the questions. But I prefer the response. Generally, in the history, we have many examples. In any times and in all the country it is a good  subject for the writers, for the scientists, the artists. Women's comportment and men's comportment is not the same. The brains are probably different. But the differences attract, are necessary. The picture is complete, and the result is wonderful. Now, the democracy is on the top, we declare the liberty, we declare the equality  for women and man, but it is really the truth

In France, despite of the rules wich oblige employers to pay women and men equally for the same job, a very big salary diffrences remain. Indeed, men earn nearly 10% more than a women for exactly the same job and assignments despite of the law!! Is it normal and fair ? No it isn't. We, woman have a lot of fights to make before to get the equality. Despite of the law, men are always thinking that we don't deserve the same salary even if we work more !! How hypocrit is it ! What an unfair fact ! We have to fight for our rights and to change minds and habits!

Are women satisfied with their roles in UK society? Not yet. There is still some way to go before there can be true gender equality in the United Kingdom.As recently as 2010, men earned, on average, over 10 percent more than women. Many women complain of a "glass ceiling" in their chosen careers. They question why, simply because of their gender, they can rise only to a certain level, and find the positions above them almost always taken by men. Gender equality is still a big issue in the UK.

One new law has been taken by our government a few years ago concerning the amount of deposit when a person contracts a lease. Since the implementation of this new law, the management cannot ask a deposit that represents more than one month rent. The French government decided to set up this law, since future tenants were usually asked to let a deposit that represents three months of rent. However, a lot of persons were not able to pay such a deposit and they didnt have any access to accommodation. Only persons with a good salary were able to pay a such amount. From a social point of view, I can understand that accommodation is a right for everybody and if management ask for three months rent as a deposit, a lot of persons will not be able to pay and in consequence, the lease will be refused to them. On the other hand, one month rent is very few as a deposit since currently, a lot of persons who leave their accommodation let it in a very bad way and sometimes, the owner has to do a lot of wroks before finding a new tenant.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: My country is member of the CEMAC: Central African Economic and monetary community. It is an international organization which counts six countries: Cameroon, Centrafrique, Congo, Gabon, Equatorial Guinea and Tchad. The CEMAC has many goals: create a very clos union between his member, delete all problems which can disturb the trade between his countries and create a real fair trade. However since my country is member of the CEMAC, we have not notice any change in our trade with the others countries. Even the agreements that we have signed are not respected. For example we have signed an agreement which give us the right to freely go to another CEMAC country without the need of any visa. The agreement is just for the paper because until today, if we want to go in a CEMAC country we still need to have a visa. Finally I think that the most important thing is not to be member of a trade agreement, but to be able to respect all agreements of this organization.

Solar power To my mind, solar power would be the most effective form of natural power to meet our city's energy needs. The city of Lyon has been through 1582 hours of sunshine during the year of 2013. We need to take advantage of that natural power in order to reduce our consumption in fossil fuels and thus to reduce our monthly bills on electricity. The cost of installing solar panels would cost around 120 000$ and we need to include high maintenance and installation cost. However we can ask subisidies to the government if we decided to implement solar panels in the region. It could be a huge opportunity for us for multiple reasons. Firstly, it will enhance our image and reputation around the country and everywhere in Europe. Secondly, we could launch a new trend in France and thus, improving the quality of life in our country. To conclude I strongly believe that solar panels are the next thing for our beloved city and I hope that we'll take the best choice.

CSR is a very controversial issue which relates to many political, economic and ethical discussions. On the one hand, some people believe that CSR is a way to make the private companies look good and helpful. At best, it would be a PR trick, a publicity stunt or a marketing device. At worse, it would be a way of demotivating workers to fight for their rights and to engage in social struggle. It's the point of view of left wing politicians. On the other hand, most people are convinced that companies have a real responsibility to the community they belong to. Companies aren't only committed to their shareholders but also to all the stakeholders with which they have to deal: workforce, clients, local authorities, people living in the neighborhood, state. Doing good for everybody, including the disadvantaged people, is the core value of the free market economy which have always been related to philanthropy. American capitalists, who set up powerful foundations, are perfect example. In fact CSR is a new word for an old concept: Charity. The time has gone when the wealthiest simply donate directly to people in need.  Time has come when legal people do what natural people did.%% Thats what CSR make possible nowadays.

In my country we dont have a lot of new buildings. The tallest building are not really tall comparatively to some buildings in others countries. We dont need to have the tallest because we need to improve our economy before. However our tallest building is called The REFER-TI. It is not a skyscraper, so not really impressive. Its main entrance is very large and open to a larger hall. It measures one hundred and twenty meters, and has twenty floors. We can see a lot of antennas on is roof. I hope that one day I will have the chance to visit a town where I could see a real skyscraper and visit it. I think it can be really impressive. I love sophisticated buildings like we can see in France or in the United State of America.

To answer this question, I would say in the first place : yes, I do believe that important companies have a big role to play in their public image and in the general behavior of consumers. Such those companies are carrying an important image and they look like examples or even references to thousands of people. For instance, let's take Apple Inc.. Steve Jobs changed the life of millions of people by developing a new vision of computer softwares with Mac. And then people starting to think differently about the Internet and new technologies. They now see it as a place where everything must be simple to use and intuitive. But Apple has also an image to keep : they must avoid such scandals as those with the Foxconn company, which is in charge of iPhone building. To finish, I would say that big companies have an important part to play. They must carry a good image of businness, and not the idea that their belong only to their shareolders. For instance, in france, the government is encouraging people to buy French products and not only products which come from abroad. CSR is a matter for all of us.

Montpellier is situated, in the South of France a few miles away from the Mediterranean coast. But the tides are not very strong, so the tidal energy is not the right renewable power to choose for this place. Alternatively, the region of Montpellier is crossed by a stong river, called "Le Lez", that might be a great potential for the local population, as well as for the environment. So, a dam could be built accross The Lez. And since Montpellier population is increasing steadily , this investment would not cost a lot, because 450000 inhabitants can easily raise $75000 project costs, as part of their local taxes. In addition, that there would be no fuel costs for this project, and the maintenance cost will be very low. Consequently, Montpellier population would use a renewable energy, for a lower cost than the energy they are using now. If I were the mayor of Montpellier, I would do my best to save my city from pollution, and thus preserve the environment. In my opinion, using hydroelectric energy in Montpellier would be a big step towards building an environmentally friendly society. It seems as simple as that, but politicians don't really practice what preach.%%

It not obvious to have the answer about this question: do companies have a responsibility to society? In fact I think there is no good answer, there is just many different point of views. For me it depends of how the companys activities impact the overall society. Actually every companys activities don't have the same impact on the society. Some have a very bad impact on the society like some big industries. They really heart the atmosphere and mess up the city. These kind of companies have a great responsibility to society. I think they should be obliged to give back to the society by contributing to the improvement of the environment, by helping students or by helping schools with computers and others staff. However others companys activities have any impact on the society. These companies have not a big responsibility to society, but they could act positively in society and have some good advertisements. We call it a win-win situation because everyone win, the company and the society.

I think that corporate social responsibility is an ethic way to live properly in this world. Many people don't have good chance during their life. Not only corporate, but private people have to think how to donate when they have enough to live. I have no share, only some money on ethic accounts. I think I have some responsibility to society. And I would like that companies share the same idea. But it is not the most of them. I will create a new society in two months. It will work around primary care in my country. I have choose the model of a cooperative to avoid shareholders and to give responsibility to each of the member. If this cooperative would win money, the benefit will be shared between the members. I hope that I will be able to convince the members to set up a CSR department. Not only to donate money, but also, to give time.

Success is often associated with financial and social success. The image that comes to my mind when I think of success is a hard-working and busy businessman, who is very rich and respected by people. I imagine a man who runs behind schedule but who has the time to show his wonderful and elegant woman during fashionable evenings. Businessmen are respected because they embody the power. On the contrary, a spiritual person like Soeur Emmanuelle, who owned nothing, was admirable because she was herself and made us aware of the essential things. I consider that Soeur Emmanuelle succeeded her personal life because she remained faithful to herself and to her deepest aspirations. She found the meaning of her life by believing in God and by helping the most needy. However, you can find some similarities between these two examples. The businessman and the spiritual person both have a goal and do their best to reach it. They both have qualities and skills that lead them to success like intelligence, a great involvement in what they do, an extraordinary strength of character and perseverance. Despite these similarities, I cant help thinking that the businessman is just gifted like thousands of businessmen whereas Soeur Emmanuelle is an exceptional human being.%%

Every day you can observe that companies are more worried about competitiveness than about respect for their employees or environment. Lately, there was a big fire in Bangladesh in which quite a lot of European and American firms were involved. How many workers, mostly women, died in this accident? How many CEO felt directly or indirectly responsible?  Dont you remember, too, the oil spills in Brittany, Alaska and Gulf of Mexico? The only law seems to be competitiveness. Market shares, shareholders dictate their rules and corporate social responsibility seems to be no more than a wishful thinking. Reality being what it is, would it be responsible to give up? There are so many powerful and gigantic international groups dominating not only economy and finance but also news and our minds that the temptation is to get desperate. Are we allowed to give up? Did Muhamad Yunus, Nobel Peace Prize, founder of the Grameen Bank give up?  In one of the poorest countries in the world, he invented new economical and successful concepts. In our own country, France, the social economy won today a true victory :Les Atelires who were supposed to be defeated, two days ago, were given enough money, from private donators, to save their jobs.%% What a symbol to celebrate the day of the women tomorrow!

The typhoon Haiyan, one of the most power which was never unregistered, it caused at least 10 000 deaths and more than 600 000 people displaced last Friday . The peak of the wind have exceeded 378km/h devastating everything on his way in The Philippines. A tropical depression going too far of pouring rains is announced on the area of Tuesday morning threatening to get worse the situation.  Haiyan has been declined moving in the West of Vietnam. The assistance have a lot of problems to reach the towns and villages in the centre of Philippines blasted and  overwhelmed by the typhoon Haiyan.  The survivors have no water, neither food nor medication and the operations of the help still difficult because the roads, airports and bridge have been destroyed by the storm or are recovered by debris. 40 tons of energetic cookies will be sent by planes to feed 120 000 people plus emergency equipments of telecommunications. The Us army will send 90 000 men from the navy based on the south of Japan.%%

One month ago, a terror attack has been place in Russia in the city of Volgograd. The death toll rose to 16 people dead. Just before the starting of the winter Olympic Games in Sotchi this week, there has been mounting critics about the organization about this controversial Olympic Games. First of all, there are a lot of critics about the cost of these games, around 50 billions dollars, which is five times larger than the organization of the Vancouver Olympic Games. Besides some experts believe that there has been a lot of corruption in the different contracts deliver by the president Poutine. Second of all, some westerns countries are worried about the treatment of gay people in Russia. Indeed, a new bill has passed recently, and gay people are not allowed to kiss each other on public squares. That is why presidents such as Barack Obama or the French president ,Francois Hollande would not attend the inaugural ceremony.

Green business is important to me. To be truly green, a business should consider using renewable energy, such as solar or wind power, or an office with a green roof. Businesses that sell green products have to conserve natural resources as much as possible by using organic, recycled and biodegradable materials in their products and packaging. I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I will pay a lot more for certain items. It's not always easy to find truly green products, and I don't want to use gasoline by driving to buy green products. However, I am concerned about the food I eat, so I sacrifice other luxuries in my life to make sure I'm eating healthy food. Of course, you have to take a company at its word that the product is truly green, which is why I buy from companies I know a lot about.

I strongly believe that when you have to search things and having the feeling of wasting your time, you lose a potential client. Furthermore, it is untrustworthy! People nowadays, order at their lunch break, and have no time to browse everything in the front of their computer. However, the website has to show us that we are making a bargain. A lot of website know how to compare all prices and to be an eye-catching to hook the future client. What if you buy a bad quality of the good , either not be able to return it? What if you have nobody who can answer to your complain? Finally, what if you have the feeling that your identity has been stolen by a con? Definitely, internet bring us a lot of comfortable way to save our time, and to compare all prices, but we should also have in our mind the possibilities to think that we might be stolen. topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Good evening and welcome everyone. I'm Francis. As we all know, we are here to celebrate Hugh and Samuella's wedding. I'd like to offer a toast to them. To start, I'd like to tell you that there love story is a youthful romance. While they still were students they already shared a common interest which is a passion for song and music. To pursue their higher education, they often had to be far from one another for long periods of time. But never has time or distance altered their relationship. Their patience and desire to well organize such an event is a good indication of their respect for marriage. Hugh and Samuella, I believe that your couple will be a source of inspiration for family, friends and colleagues. I wish you to have as many children as you want and to be successful in your assignments for the generation we're living in. Could we please all stand up and raise our glasses to Hugh and Samuella!

During the latest 30 years of the XXth. century, many famous landmarks were built in Paris: Centre Georges Pompidou, Montparnasse Tower, Louvre Pyramid, and Bastille Opera. Some of them were very controversial. But one thing is certain, they all will left a long lasting footprint in the urban landscape. In my opinion, the Centre Georges Pompidou is the most obvious failure. Its groundbreaking architecture and its garish aggressive colors are unnecessarily provocative and upsetting. In opposite, the Louvre Pyramid is certainly an unquestionable achievement. This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

Well, I gave a look at the list of propositions we had from some companies to choose a kind of renewable energy for our city. I choose wind power. The reason why I choosed that particular kind of energy is for several reasons : First, we here live by the sea, and the city is build alongside the shores, and we have wind all days. Second, the impact of the installation on the environment is quite low, so we could think about put turbines in many different places without have the fear of destructing the ecosystem if we do that. But I know, the cost is non-negligible (80000$), but I think if we do this, in less than ten years we should be able to reduce our cost of electricity by twice and save up more money to build new ecological facilities in town. It's a bet, but I'm sure everybody here is willing to do this if this is the better way we find to reduce the footprint of our city and habitants.

Although companies play an important role in developing economy, they do have a responsibility to society. For example, if a company owns a plant near a residential area, it has to comply with environmental protection standards. So that the area will remain clean and the population will stay healthy. It is not wise to think that company's only responsibility is to its shareholders. That is not win to win situation, because the main concern in this case is making money. So the company focuses on the most effective strategy to make money, even if that harms the environment and does not respect human rights. Instead of re-investing the whole company's turnover, money should be used in providing employees with good working conditions. Many companies in my country have a good sense of CSR. They save energy by installing solar panels on their plants' roofs, so they use it to run their machinery, and sell some to power supply companies. The saved money in energy is therefore used to reward the company's employees, for their hard work, and thus motivate them.

Although France is known as the country of human rights, French law is quite strict towards hardened criminals; for example, individuals detaining  weapons without any convincible purpose, and with no legal authorization from the local authorities, they will be arrested and emprisoned. Since the 11th of September 2001, France has been taking the same measures as many others countries, as part of global war on terror. Security forces have been using the most efficient tools to track terrorists; any suspects should be arrested for investigation. If there are enough evidence against them, they will be convicted and jailed. As for murderers, they are sentenced to life emprisonment, because the death penalty was abolished in France, in 1981. However, shoplifters and purse-snatchers are apprehended, but they may be released a few hours later, if the victims do not wish to take them to court. Unlike the security forces, French law is too lenient towards juvenile delinquency and petty crimes. So young offenders are generally recidivist, and are likely to become hardened criminals after many years of experience. Therefore, a solution should be found to reduce recidivism, and thus combat criminality at source.

It seems to me that successful people have many points in common. First, they live their lives in a positive way. They never stand too long on negative points and always think about what should be the next step to succeed. Whatever happens to them, they are ready to bounce back. In addition to that, they are persistant, passionate and ready to adapt themselves (or their ideas) to the market's needs. Of course, they also have a strong need to be their own boss and need to do things in their own way. However, sometimes, you can meet people who have successful people's traits and habits but are considered by other people as "loosers". Yet, when you talk to them, you are surprised to see they still believe in themselves, feeling that one day their turn will come up and they will be successful. To do so, they will need the only thing you have to get to be successful : the idea ! Without it, they will continue to be a successful person only in their own fantasy world.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Jos, The visit to Portugal with CDMR is now clearly set. We will arrived on Monday evening in Lisbonne, in order to be ready to go Tuesday morning. First, Cristina will bring us to Artebel for technical visit of the plant. After lunch, we will go to Avelar with Eduardo. It would be a short and efficient visit of the expanded clay plant's devices. I live in the country where no buldings have been built. Only small houses and farms... But my village is not so far from a big city : Lyon in France. Lyon is the second city in France after Paris. A quarter is famous in Lyon : the Part-Dieu. It is famous because it is considered as the second business place in France. There is a lot of buildings and a few skyscrapers. The best wellknowed was called the Credit Lyonnais tour, then the "Tour de la Part Dieu" . The habitants call it : the pencil, because top is a pyramid. It is a red and circular building. It is 165 meter high. The seven highest floors are occupied by an hotel. The second skyscraper in this city is the tour Oxygen. I like the architecture of this other building. It is only 115 meter high, but 80% of the surface is made with glass. It is really beautiful. Even if I prefer the country...

I think my town is a masterpiece of the 14th century's architecture. In fact, I live in Avignon, France, where you can found some amazing buildings. First, the Pope's Palace, a huge castle inside the town, where popes used to live in the XIVth century. It is a very touristic  and a wonderful place that you can visit. On the top of the palace, there is a gold statue of virgin Marie, which shows the story of my town and which is particularly beautiful. The front of the monument is impressive and the park just above the palace, on a hill, is as wonderful. It's a relaxing place where I love to go with my friends or family. In Avignon, there are also a lot of small streets, really old and welcoming and some other parks with trees and benches. The library is an old church which has been renovated so we can feel secure and relax there. In fact, you don't need to love ancient architecture to love Avignon, because you can't think this town's architecture is not great, that's why a lot of people come here quite often!

At the beginning, Paris (capitale of France) was a small village. Since the middle age, most French kings wanted to make their mark on this town which has never been destroyed. Paris houses then an impressive number of historical and famous monuments which are worth visiting. What also characterises Paris are rows of buildings along tree-lined avenues.Those five-story buildings are well known for their maintained facades and their balconies on the fifth floor. In the 1830's, Rambuteau conceived this architecture with wide boulevards and large avenues in order to have the main areas cleared and to make public transport easier. Afterwards, the plan was developed by Haussmann. In addition to those typical neighbourhoods of Paris, no building better symbolises the capital of France than the Eiffel Tower. Indeed, you couldn't possibly visit Paris without seeing the Eiffel Tower which was built for the 1889 International Exhibition. The tower which was the highest structure in the world from 1889 until 1930 rises 300 meters. You can see its top from all over Paris.

Corporate social responsibility most commonly abbreviate to CSR seems to be the buzzword in management recently. A large number of companies are starting to dedicate a lot of ressources to adress the issue of CSR, such as l'Oreal which is one the pionner companies as regards to this divisive issue. However, some people believe that the only responsibility of a company is to its shareholders because they are bringing the capital to the firm.Today, it is too difficult for companies to just focus on their shareholders due to differents factors. Firstly, the negligence of corporate social responsibiliy can have a negative impact on the company's reputation.Hence it could have an impact on the future turnover of the firm.Furthermore, customers nowadays especially in modern countries seem to take a lot of attention to the company's image. This is why corporations need to deal seriously with problems such as environment, low-pay salaries or diversity.

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: In my country communication is more or less indirect. We do'nt do too much gesture when we are speaking. But what we French do, is hugging people we meet ; not at work but in the street. We also hug our friends friends also when we see them for the first time. Living at the German border, I must say , for them it's not a good behavior hugging. At work I have much contact to foreign people who have differents forms of body language. For example the Italiens are the ones which speak with theirs hands; they can't talk to you without them. For them it's also common to call me with my first name and to put a hand on my shoulder as if they know me since a long time. I like them because they are sympatic, even if I am not appreciating physical contact. I prefer their behavior to the North German's who are too cold for me. You have to worm information out of them. This can sometimes lead to misunderstandings, thinking being curious. To sumarize body language can be positif but it make you unconfortable in some situations.

In my opinion, wind power would be the most effective form of natural power to meet our city's energy needs. As you know, our city is located by the sea. We have three hundred and sixty-five windy days a year. Wind power creates no waste and does not use any fossil fuels or other kinds of finite energy. It is low-cost and highly profitable. The cost of installing wind turbines would come to only 80,000 dollars, and the maintenance costs would be low. It would be better than power, as it creates less carbon, and it would be more efficient than using a "Natural living machine" because the wind turbines would need fewer people to look after them, and therefore would be more cost-effective. All in all, as far as I'm concerned, wind power would be the most environmentally friendly and economically viable way to produce renewable energy for our city.

The buildings of Shanghai are simply breathtaking in their size and diversity. The city has an impressive number of modern skyscrapers  more than a thousand, according to some counts  that are in sharp contrast to several styles of architecture from different time periods. The Bund offers the perfect tour of sophisticated European buildings, many constructed in the mid-1800s to early 1900s in various gorgeous styles, including Renaissance, Baroque and neo-classical. The area is attractively situated along the Huangpu River. Look across the Huangpu for one of the city's most intriguing architectural contrasts: Pudong, with buildings that look like they belong in a science-fiction movie. Here, you'll immediately notice Shanghai's most famous building, the Oriental Pearl Tower. It's definitely not the world's most beautiful building, but it is one of the tallest (at 468 meters) and most unusual. The spire and antennas reach an impressive 118 meters high. Near the Pearl is the Shanghai World Financial Center, which, with 101 floors, is China's tallest building.

In one hand, there are petty crimes, purse snatching and shoplifting, often occurred by young people, those lead to juvenile detention. In the other hand there are violent crimes: hardened criminals, murderer, terrorist which can not be considered the same way and often lead to incarceration. The juvenile detention gives higher chance to young people for rehabilitation than incarceration, provided that young people can get a new or different education in these special centers. The main goal of incarceration is to protect society from violent criminals activities and second to prepare criminals to their rehabilitation. But the chances of success are smaller as violent criminals are not committed to renew their education, consequently hardened criminals might be considered beyond hope. As far as I am concerned, crime could be considered differently in my country: even if the differentiation between petty crimes and violent crimes is clearly made in the court, but unless there is a lack of juvenile detention centers, young people can be incarcerate as well as hardened criminals. The consequences are disaster as recidivism gets higher and juvenile criminals get hardened.

I think, one of the most interesting and effective management strategy is to use the SWOT analysis technique, that is exactly what our business need. SWOT helps evaluation of internal and external strengths,weaknesses, opportunities and threats of a business. We need to have everything clear and in order if we want the SWOT analysis technique works well. We need to work more time on the team to identify the strengths in order to motivate the team and also identifying the weaknesses helps to be aware of the problems in the future. Knowing how the team works we can pay attention on how we can make movements and task assignments, we can learn how to motivate the team, make them stronger and develop mutual trust, so the team can get involve in the process, collaborating altogether to complete the SWOT analysis. Making changes to this technique could be a good idea to take advantage of all the features it offers.

I graduated from the university with specializing in production systems 22 years ago. I started my career working as production supervisor in an advertising company. Afterward, changed to a medical company as a manufacturing engineer. Over half of my working life, I have been working in that medical company where I performed multiple roles. My last role before leaving the company was as manufacturing manager. Presently, I am as manager of molding in a very important electronics company. Although I have very little time in this new job, the company is large and very important. I have great confidence that in a short time, I'll have excellent opportunities for professional development in my new company. One of my biggest challenges is to be plant manager, and to achieve it I continued getting ready. I am currently studying a masters in finance. I hope to achieve it maximum in five years. Another of my future plans is to start my own business, but that is only in plans.

In my country there are many traditional shows and others coming from other countries. It is hard to say which is better because there are very good Shows these days. But undoubtedly there is a very traditional show in my country which has hundreds of years staged. It is known worldwide as the dance of the deer, and this staging tells the story of a hunter goes into the forest looking for his next medal which is a great deer. Both the hunter and the deer are played by men wearing clothing according to their role. The story is wrapped in music and dances where second by second narrate how the story takes place. The plot is very deep because talks about hatred that hunter feels for the deer. At the end, it is amazing how the hunter when facing his legendary and dying enemy aims to communicate to the audience all unexpected feelings of anguish and pain to see his enemy agonize.

Has gender equality improved in Mexico in recent years? It's interesting that many people believe that it has, but we can still see many gaps in work opportunities as well as in politics roles. Mexico has never had a woman as a President, even as governor of any state, woman in the senate only account for the 20%, which is really low compared to the actual gender population Mix (52% women and 48% men). There is a law that the Political Parties have to postulate at least 40% women, but most of them, if they won the election, are then replaced for Men. Still much to do in Mexico to make gender equality improves.

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: English is truly important in the Mexican culture given that we have a common border with the United States and the trade between both countries is enormous, but also there are many US Companies operating in Mexico requiring a fluent English in their corporate positions. Talking about education, the most recognize private schools (Public education in Mexico is unfortunately very poor) in the country included as a part of their programs English lessons. In my current position in the Financial Team in a leading Telecommunications Company, English is needed, all the Major Banks in the Country have their Headquarters in U.S, so main meetings are driving in English and if you need additional funds not available with the banks you should find those in the Capital Markets where investors, Accounting Firms, Lawyers, Rating Agencies, Regulators are all located in US.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: I recently attended a presentation about our sales strategies. The presenter was Rafael Cuevas the new Sales Manager. I have to admire how the calm the presenter was. His voice was strong as he emphasized the key points. And I have to admire his excellent speaking ability. One thing he did well was to deliver an inspiring conclusion. I think that he made us all feel motivated to go get new clients after the presentation. However, the principal goal was so diffuse; his flowcharts were confusing, they didnt help make all the sales procedure clear. In fact, the board of directors was angry about that. Overall, the new sales manager is motivated, unfortunately he doesnt know the kind of business that the company have. The CFO recommended him a better presentation. The CEO suggested he should visit each branch to know all about the company. I guess he will improve for the next meeting.

In Mexico manners are different according at geographic zone that are you living or visiting. In south region is a common practice offer a small gift on birthdays, gifts are related to gender. For example if you are visiting a woman, flowers are accepted, for a man wine will be ok,  in some places also small bucket of fruit. But in the north things are totally different, most common practice is a money on a birthday card is accepted. Visiting a friend house is formal to call  at least one day before, is not polite just arrive on the last minute. Other important rules are on a restaurant appointments. Is not polite to arrive late on any date, a lot of people think that arrive 15 or 20 minutes late looks good, but not. Other common practice on a restaurant is order a center dish for all the attendances, if you are thinking to do that just be sure that all people like the food that are you thinking, for example some people hate onions, others can't eat cheese.

text: I recently attended an interesting talk about why we can be sure about a coming better future. I want to highlight how confident the speaker was. It is worthy that he was well prepared. He choses viewpoint question to help people to reason at heart instead of just giving endless information. Also, he uses short and effective phrases, then, the information was well supported and easy to memorize. When I was younger I had two embarrasing moments. My first moment was when I were at school with my girlfriends talking about silly things and drinking soda. I felt so out of place and blushed myself. Oh my Gosh! My second awkward moment was when I were in a place called the Rodeo Santa Fe where you can dance country music. I were with my friends having fun and then the cheerleader made a contest where you have to drink a big glass of beer, turn around a coin in order to get you dizzy and run to the other side of the dance floor to win the competition. So I participate in the contest, drank my big glass of beer, turned it around the coin but whe I ran to the other side I didn't run straigth ahead and I fall over a table... it was the most awkward moment I've neen trough.

text: I like watching snowboarding, especially when there are olympics games, but I would not practice, i think its extremely dangerous. The sport I love to practice it's rock climbing, I think it's exciting, climbing steep slopes and find that your hands are the only ones that separates you from a free fall it's like... i don't know how to explain it, i'm speechless, well of course the harness will be there if you have a slip and  the leg straps that are a full salvation. The first time I went climbing, i was sweating profusely, it was really a wall, but I could hear the thud of my heart beat, then I felt cramps in the fingers, I let go ... i felt like I fell from a great height, it was the most terrifying experience of my life, I felt very scared and didn't want to do it again, but I had to face that fear and a few months later, I returned, very scared, but eager to keep climbing, I love this sport.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA, or the North American Free Trade Agreement, was created 20 years ago to expand trade between the U.S., Canada and Mexico. It's secondary purpose was to make these countries more competitive in the global marketplace. It has been wildly successful in achieving this. NAFTA is now the largest free trade agreement in the world . One of the problems with NAFTA is that it's reduced U.S. jobs. A second disadvantage is that it has exploited Mexico's farmers and its environment .Rural Mexican farmers could not compete with low-cost corn and other grains that were exported by subsidized U.S. farm corporations. NAFTA put more than a million Mexican farmers were out of business. The ones that remained were forced to use more fertilizers and farm marginal land, resulting in more pollution and deforestation. Labor was cheap in Mexico because they had no labor rights or health protection. Thanks to NAFTA, nearly a third of Mexico's labor force were in this "maquiladora" program. I live in The City of Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico. I grew up with this program and I find this so unfair. NAFTA called for unlimited transportation between the three member countries. This would have created a dangerous situation, since Mexican trucks were not up to U.%%S. safety standards, and Mexican roads could not have carried the heavier U.S. trucks. This problem was averted in 2008, when Congress prohibited this portion of NAFTA from ever being carried out

Corporate social responsibility its more and more important for companies. Since companies are or have CSR can develop employee satisfaction. By definition, CSR is active and voluntary social, economic and environmental improvement contributions by companies, usually the goal is to improve their competitive position and value-added value. The overall assessment system organizational performance in these areas is known as the triple bottom line. he main ethical responsibilities of the company with the workers and the community are: Serving society with useful products and in fair conditions. Creating wealth in the most efficient way possible. Respect human rights with decent working conditions which promote safety and health and human development and training of workers. Ensure the continuity of the company and if it possible, achieve reasonable growth . Respect the environment wherever possible avoiding any contamination by minimizing the generation of waste and more efficient use of natural and energy resources. Strictly enforced the laws , regulations, rules and customs, respecting the legitimate contracts and commitments . Maintenance of business ethics and anti-corruption . In order to mention someones...

Erasing unwanted memories would sound crazy. But is now possible using the electroconvulsive therapy (ETC). Researchers found using this technique the person will forget troubling memories. This method was used to treat severe cases of major depression.  ECT induces seizures in the brain. It isnt clear why it works to alleviate symptoms of major depression, but it is so effective. The research published in Nature was conducted with people undergoing ECT for major depression. They were told very troubling stories. For example one about a child whose feet were amputated. A week later, just prior to receiving ECT, they were asked to recall these stories. The idea was that remembering the details would shift the story into a very recent form of recall, where it might be temporarily unstable. This may mean that people who have gone through terrible trauma in war or in accidents can have the memory of those events removed by bringing them to mind and then using ECT to erase them.

It's difficult to talk about Corporate Social Responsibility because if we consider that nowadays we are living in an era of globalization and multinational corporations; most of them' obviously are focused on generating profits. However, as result of that situation, they are affecting the environment and the social welfare as well. Fortunately, at the present more and more companies are adopting CSR, although some people say that it's just a marketing device used for companies to improve reputation of their brands. Particularly, I think corporations should not be only responsible to their shareholders; its important to have ethical principles and moral to conduct business in the wider community and make good for the society, but there are powerful arguments on both sides of the issue. On the one hand, companies only are responsible to its shareholders, but on the other hand, community is letting them to do business. For that reason they should contribute to the sustainability of the environment and support the local community by taking actions based on ethical values for the benefit of society.

I think to be a successful person you need a lot of traits, but an extra thing that you need is good luck. However, you should to work on it, because you wont find the success in front of you. So, how do you can achieve your personal goals and become in a successful person? Most important, you have to be a persistent person. Think about what it is that you really want to do with your life, and go for it. Once you know what you want, stay focused. Don't let anything or anyone stand in your way. Don't take 'no' for an answer or let failure or mistakes along the way stop you from realizing your objective. It's your life and your dream, be it big or small. You will find a lot of obstacles during the way; take them like an opportunity to learn and avoid them the next time. Good luck.

Crime is among the most urgent concerns in Mexico, as a Mexican I'm very worried. Mexico has experienced increasingly high crime rates, especially in major urban centers, and is repeatedly marked by violence. I consider that drug trafficking were the main problem because it is the cause of another crimes as kidnapping, violent robbery, extortion, car thefts etc. The Government has changed some laws in order to reduce the crime, for example the sentence for a kidnapper or a blackmailer is a life sentence. Another thing that the Government did was the installation of thousands of security cameras around the city and a very large expansion of the city police force nowadays Mexico City has one of the highest police officers in the world with one uniformed police officer per every one hundred citizens, with this increase, violent and petty crime rates both falling significantly despite the rise in violent crime in other parts of the country.

There are three building in Mxico taller than 200 meters, two of them are located in Mexico City and the other one in Guadalajara: Torre Mayor (Main Tower), Torre Ejecutiva Pemex (Pemex Executive Tower) and the Riu Hotel . All of the buildings are measured from the main entrance to the top and include the spires, but not the antennas. Personally, I like buildings with character, more than tall buildings of course, this doesnt apply to New York, which I consider one of the most beautiful cities in the world with all the skyscrapers. Therefore, I like  buildings like museums, with their own personality. In Monterrey, my city, there is Marco, the Contemporary Museum of Art, which, despite the impressive collection of art that it houses, is a masterpiece in itself, with the solid structures, patio, and with all the elements that take us to the colonial times. I like also a beautiful hotel located in Mexico City (Camino Real Polanco) because its architecture includes various Mexican elements such as the patios, the tall windows and ceilings, bright colors and very vivid ambiance.

Mexico City is a blend between the history buildings and the contemporary ones. Mexico is a city created for the spanish people who came here in the 15Th century. They wanted to construct here the new Spain and because of that, they built over the arquitecture of the aztecs. Nowadays we can admire, mostly in the center of the city the ruins of the Tenochtitln city (this is the name of the ancient city). In the old city there was a lot of religious temples but they were covered with catholic churches. In this century we have modern buildings in some places of the city, so like I said before you can admire both. If you come here I recomend you to take the "turibus" this is a bus which can take you to different places of the city and they explain you the history of them and you can learn a lot of it. You can down of the bus where ever you want, it has a lot of stops around the city, but I warn you, this is one of the largest cities in the world !

My town used to be attended by local grocery stores I mean, mom-and-dad business for many years, but on 1994 was the year of globalization, due a international agreement that the government made between Canada and the U.S., so many big-box stores started to settle on the city and the people welcomed them in a positive way, it was the hip by then, everybody wanted to do shopping on these super-stores, due to the TV spots, as I was saying, all of these big-box stores hired many people and at first everything seemed to be good, because all the people had a job, and by then low vandalism activities were reported, and low bad social behaviors reports were almost cero. These two bad activities started to increase due that many people started to immigrate from different parts of the country, because of the high demand of work force by then, nowadays our city has growth in a high way, that now we have a lot of work force, instead of work demand, and this effect brought our city high poverty index,  low paid work positions and all mom-and-dad business are closed, due that they couldn't compete to these big-box stores
